title: Merge shell groups into matching-label agent groups (D1/D2)
action: Implement D1/D2 in buildHomeGroups (web/src/views/switcher.ts:89-115): after building agentGroups and shellById exactly as today, merge each shell-only group into the agent group whose workspace_label string is exactly equal to the shell group's own workspace_label, appending the shell group's rows onto that agent group's rows array. The merged group MUST keep the agent group's own (non-null) workspace_status -- never overwrite it with the shell group's null. A shell group whose label matches no agent group's label stays its own separate group, unchanged from today. Update the doc comment currently at switcher.ts:81-88 -- it claims shell rows can never land in a group that has an agent card, which becomes false after this change; rewrite it to describe the actual label-match merge and its accepted collision risk (per D1's rationale in CONTEXT.md), citing D1/D2. In web/test/switcher.test.ts's existing `describe("buildHomeGroups", ...)` block: update the existing test's description so it's scoped to the differing-label case (it currently claims shells and agents are unconditionally split into separate groups), and add a new test named exactly `merges a shell group into the agent group sharing its workspace_label` that builds one agent row and one shell row with different workspace_id values but the identical workspace_label, asserts the result is a single HomeGroup containing both an agent row and a shell row, and asserts that group's workspace_status equals the agent's own status (not null).

truths: buildHomeGroups merges a shell-only group into an agent group when their workspace_label strings are exactly equal, even when their workspace_id values differ
A merged group's workspace_status is the agent group's own non-null status, never overwritten by the shell group's null
A shell group whose label matches no agent group remains its own separate group, unchanged from today's behavior

artifacts.substantive: buildHomeGroups performs the label-match merge; the doc comment above it accurately describes the merge instead of claiming shells never share a group with an agent
existing separate-group test description scoped to differing labels; new test 'merges a shell group into the agent group sharing its workspace_label' added and passing

key_links: buildHomeGroups's returned HomeGroup array places a label-matched shell group's rows inside the corresponding agent group's rows
prohibitions: No change to src/web/api.rs or any backend/server-side filtering -- this is frontend-only per D1
No change to groupByWorkspace or agent-only grouping behavior

verify: cd web && grep -q "merges a shell group into the agent group sharing its workspace_label" test/switcher.test.ts && npm run typecheck && npm run test -- --run switcher.test.ts

trace.outcome: buildHomeGroups (switcher.ts) now merges a shell-only group into the agent group sharing its exact workspace_label, appending shell rows onto the agent group's rows and keeping the agent's non-null workspace_status; doc comment at switcher.ts:81-96 rewritten to describe the merge and its D1 collision risk; existing buildHomeGroups test rescoped to the differing-label case, new test added for the merge case.
trace.verify_output: typecheck: tsc --noEmit clean. vitest: Test Files 1 passed (1), Tests 19 passed (19), Duration 760ms.
trace.verify_command: cd web && grep -q "merges a shell group into the agent group sharing its workspace_label" test/switcher.test.ts && npm run typecheck && npm run test -- --run switcher.test.ts
